VesselFinder

📊 Methodology & Analysis Logic

×

1. Route Detection Algorithm

Routes are automatically detected from raw AIS position data using the following logic:

  • Stop Detection: A "stop" is defined as a vessel being stationary (speed < 1.0 kn) or having a status of 'Moored'/'Anchored' for at least 30 minutes.
  • Port Matching: Stops are matched to known ports if they occur within 1,000 meters (1 km) of a port's center point.
  • Valid Trip: A movement between two different ports is counted as a "trip" only if the distance traveled is greater than 100 km. This filters out local movements.

2. Statistical Definitions

Draught (depth) statistics are calculated based on port stops, not open-sea readings:

  • For each trip, we take the maximum draught recorded at either the Origin or Destination port stop (max(origin_max, dest_max)). This represents the "Trip Draught".
  • The statistics below summarize these "Trip Draught" values across all trips on the route:
Min Draught
Lightest Trip
The lowest "Trip Draught" observed on this route.
Avg Draught
Average
The average of all "Trip Draught" values.
Max Draught
Heaviest Trip
The highest "Trip Draught" observed on this route.

3. Data Interpretation

Metric Description
Total Trips Number of valid port-to-port journeys detected (>100km).
Route Count Unique Origin -> Destination pairs.
Ship Types Analysis is filtered to only include Cargo and Tanker vessels.
Based on script: analyze_routes.py • Minimum Trip Distance: 100km • Stop Duration: >30min
